name: Home & Cleaning
tagline: Little and often beats the weekend panic-clean.

templates.title: The Daily Reset
templates.blurb: Two ten-minute loops that keep the house permanently 'good enough for guests'.

steps.label: Spray and wipe the kitchen
steps.note: Spray first, let it sit while you tidy

steps.label: Quick sweep of the busy floors

templates.title: Weekend Deep Clean
templates.blurb: One focused Saturday session for the jobs the daily loop doesn't touch.

routines.title: Saturday blitz
routines.footer: Never mix bleach with ammonia or vinegar — the fumes are dangerous.

steps.label: Spray the bathroom and let it soak
steps.note: Dwell time does the scrubbing for you

questions.prompt: What does home look like right now?

options.title: Fine until you open a cupboard
Tidy — I want to keep it that way

questions.prompt: When do you actually have energy to clean?

educationPages.title: Dwell time
educationPages.body: Cleaners need contact time to work — spray, walk away for five minutes, then wipe. Half the scrubbing you do is just compensating for wiping too soon.

educationPages.title: Top to bottom
educationPages.body: Dust falls. Clean shelves before counters, counters before floors, and you'll never re-clean the same surface twice in one session.

educationPages.title: Little and often
educationPages.body: A ten-minute daily reset prevents the four-hour weekend ordeal. Mess grows exponentially — the cheapest time to clean anything is right now, while it's small.

educationPages.title: Never mix chemicals
educationPages.body: Bleach plus ammonia (many glass cleaners) or bleach plus vinegar makes toxic gas. One product at a time, rinse between products, and keep the window open.

products.name: Microfiber cloths
products.what: Washable cloths whose split fibers grab dust and grease instead of pushing them around.
products.why: One damp microfiber replaces most paper towels and half your sprays.
products.order: Everywhere — colour-code them: kitchen, bath, glass.

products.name: All-purpose cleaner
products.what: A neutral cleaner safe for most sealed surfaces.
products.why: One bottle for counters, tables and spills keeps the daily loop simple.
products.caution: Never combine with bleach or other cleaners; test delicate surfaces first.

products.name: Shower squeegee
products.what: A rubber blade that pulls water off glass and tile.
products.why: Thirty seconds after each shower prevents the limescale you'd otherwise scrub for an hour.
products.order: Last step of every shower — hang it in the stall.
